name: Calangute Beach
description: Calangute Beach is the largest and most popular beach in North Goa, known for its vibrant shacks, water sports like parasailing and jet skiing, and lively nightlife.

name: Fort Aguada
description: Fort Aguada is a well-preserved 17th-century Portuguese fort overlooking the Arabian Sea with a four-storey lighthouse and panoramic coastline views.

name: Baga Beach
description: Baga Beach is famous for its nightlife, beach clubs like Tito's and Mambo's, and water sports. The Saturday Night Market nearby offers great shopping.

name: Anjuna Flea Market
description: The Anjuna Flea Market is a famous Wednesday market offering handicrafts, jewelry, spices, and clothing with live music and food stalls.

name: Basilica of Bom Jesus
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site housing the mortal remains of St. Francis Xavier. Built in 1605, it is one of the finest examples of baroque architecture in India.

name: Dudhsagar Waterfalls
description: Dudhsagar Falls is a four-tiered waterfall at 310 meters. A jeep safari through Bhagwan Mahavir Wildlife Sanctuary leads to the falls.

name: Red Fort
description: The Red Fort is a UNESCO World Heritage Site built by Shah Jahan in 1638 with museums, Diwan-i-Aam, Diwan-i-Khas, and nightly Sound and Light show.

name: Qutub Minar
description: Qutub Minar is a 73-meter tall UNESCO World Heritage Site, the tallest brick minaret in the world, built in 1193.

name: India Gate
description: India Gate is a war memorial dedicated to 70,000 Indian soldiers who died in World War I. Beautifully illuminated at night.

name: Chandni Chowk
description: One of the oldest and busiest markets in Old Delhi, famous for street food, spices at Khari Baoli, and electronics.

name: Humayun's Tomb
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and the first garden-tomb on the Indian subcontinent, inspiring the design of the Taj Mahal.

name: Lotus Temple
description: The Lotus Temple is a Bahai House of Worship shaped like a lotus flower with 27 marble petals, welcoming people of all faiths.

name: Amber Fort
description: Amber Fort is a majestic hilltop fort built in 1592 with Sheesh Mahal, Diwan-i-Aam, and Ganesh Pol gate.

name: Hawa Mahal
description: Hawa Mahal, the Palace of Winds, is a five-storey pink sandstone structure with 953 small windows built in 1799.

name: City Palace
description: The City Palace blends Rajasthani and Mughal architecture with museums, royal costumes, and armory collections.

name: Jantar Mantar
description: A UNESCO World Heritage astronomical observation site with the world's largest stone sundial.

name: Nahargarh Fort
description: Nahargarh Fort on the Aravalli Hills offers breathtaking sunset views of Jaipur city with a wax museum and rooftop restaurant.

name: Johari Bazaar
description: Jaipur's most famous jewelry market for Kundan, Meenakari, gemstone jewelry, and Rajasthani textiles.

name: Alleppey Backwaters
description: Alleppey is the Venice of the East, famous for houseboat cruises through palm-fringed backwater canals and lagoons.

name: Munnar Tea Gardens
description: Munnar is a hill station at 1,600m with sprawling tea plantations, misty mountains, and the Tata Tea Museum.

name: Fort Kochi
description: Fort Kochi reflects Portuguese, Dutch, and British colonial influences with Chinese Fishing Nets and St. Francis Church.

name: Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary
description: One of India's finest wildlife reserves in Thekkady with boat rides offering sightings of elephants and bison.

name: Varkala Beach
description: Varkala Beach is unique for its dramatic cliff backdrop with the 2,000-year-old Janardanaswamy Temple nearby.

name: Solang Valley
description: Solang Valley offers skiing in winter and paragliding, zorbing in summer, surrounded by snow-capped peaks.

name: Rohtang Pass
description: Rohtang Pass at 3,978m offers stunning views of glaciers and peaks. Permit required from Manali SDM office.

name: Hadimba Temple
description: A unique 1553 wooden temple dedicated to Hidimbi Devi set amidst tall deodar trees with pagoda-style architecture.

name: Old Manali
description: Old Manali is a charming village known for bohemian cafes, bakeries, handicraft shops, and the Manu Temple.

name: Jogini Waterfall
description: A beautiful 150-foot cascade reached via a 3km trek through apple orchards and pine forests.

name: Dashashwamedh Ghat
description: The main ghat with the famous Ganga Aarti ceremony every evening at 7 PM with fire rituals and chanting.

name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
description: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas, the most famous Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva with the new Kashi Vishwanath Corridor.

name: Sarnath
description: Where Lord Buddha gave his first sermon. Features the Dhamek Stupa, Ashoka Pillar, and Sarnath Museum.

name: Boat Ride on Ganges
description: An early morning boat ride witnessing ghats coming alive with pilgrims, yoga practitioners, and the sunrise.

name: Assi Ghat
description: The southernmost ghat where the Assi River meets the Ganges, popular for morning yoga and cultural performances.

name: BHU & Bharat Kala Bhavan
description: Banaras Hindu University campus with the Bharat Kala Bhavan museum housing over 100,000 artifacts.

name: Gateway of India
description: Iconic arch monument built in 1924 overlooking the Arabian Sea. Ferries to Elephanta Caves depart from here.

name: Marine Drive
description: A 3.6km arc-shaped boulevard along the coastline, nicknamed the Queen's Necklace for its glittering night lights.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: UNESCO World Heritage rock-cut cave temples on Elephanta Island dedicated to Lord Shiva with the 7th-century Trimurti sculpture.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the most magnificent Victorian Gothic railway stations in the world.

name: Colaba Causeway Market
description: Mumbai's most famous street market for clothes, jewelry, antiques, and handicrafts near the Taj Hotel.

name: Juhu Beach
description: Mumbai's most popular beach famous for street food stalls serving pav bhaji, bhel puri, and vada pav.

name: City Palace Udaipur
description: The largest palace complex in Rajasthan overlooking Lake Pichola with museums, courtyards, and peacock mosaics.

name: Lake Pichola Boat Ride
description: A boat ride offering stunning views of City Palace, Jag Mandir, and the Lake Palace. Sunset rides are magical.

name: Jagdish Temple
description: A beautifully carved Indo-Aryan temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu built in 1651 with intricate wall carvings.

name: Saheliyon Ki Bari
description: Garden of Maidens with lotus pools, marble elephants, and cascading fountains built for 48 royal maidens.

name: Fateh Sagar Lake
description: An artificial lake surrounded by hills with Nehru Garden on an island, popular for boating and evening walks.

name: Haldighati
description: The historic mountain pass of the Battle of Haldighati with the Maharana Pratap Museum and Chetak Samadhi.

name: Laxman Jhula
description: An iconic iron suspension bridge over the Ganges built in 1929 connecting Tapovan to Jonk with temples on both sides.

name: River Rafting
description: White water rafting with Grade 3-4 rapids. The 16km Shivpuri to Laxman Jhula stretch is the most popular.

name: Beatles Ashram
description: Where the Beatles stayed in 1968 for transcendental meditation. Features meditation caves and graffiti art galleries.

name: Triveni Ghat
description: The most sacred bathing ghat where Ganga, Yamuna, and Saraswati are believed to meet. Beautiful evening Ganga Aarti.

name: Neer Garh Waterfall
description: A two-tiered waterfall 5km from Laxman Jhula with natural rock pools perfect for swimming.

name: Taj Mahal
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the Seven Wonders. Built by Shah Jahan for Mumtaz Mahal in white marble.

name: Agra Fort
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site built by Emperor Akbar in 1565. Offers beautiful Taj Mahal views from Musamman Burj.

name: Fatehpur Sikri
description: UNESCO World Heritage ghost city built by Akbar in 1571 with the Buland Darwaza, the tallest gateway in the world.

name: Mehtab Bagh
description: A charbagh garden opposite the Taj Mahal offering the most stunning sunset views, far less crowded.

name: Kinari Bazaar
description: Agra's oldest market famous for marble inlay work, leather goods, zardozi embroidery, and petha sweets.
